action.reasoning: Observation: beam/length-penalty sweep on the current best checkpoint shows no gain beyond beam 8–12 and alpha 0.2, with val exprate staying around 0.0436 versus the best recorded 0.0446. Analysis: decoding is not the main bottleneck now; model quality is. The previous continuation from 16 to 24 epochs improved test exprate from 0.025 to 0.030, so a shorter additional continuation from the current best checkpoint at lower LR is the highest-ROI move. With ~3.5h left, a +6 epoch run is affordable and leaves room for one more eval/iteration if needed. Decision: keep architecture unchanged, train 6 more epochs from best checkpoint with reduced LR and beam-10 validation, then submit eval using the updated best checkpoint.
